// Code Generated by Sidekick is for learning and experimentation purposes only.

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Diagnostics;
using System.Linq;
using System.Text;
using System.Threading.Tasks;

namespace EntityAttribute_POC
{
    public class TestOrchestrator
    {
        private readonly string _projectPath;   // full path to .csproj
        private readonly string _projectFolder; // folder containing .csproj
        private readonly string? _testCasePath;
        private readonly LLMCodeMigrator _migrator;

        public TestOrchestrator(string projectPath, string? testCasePath = null)
        {
            _migrator = LLMCodeMigrator.TryCreate(EventData);
            _projectPath = projectPath;
            _projectFolder = Path.GetDirectoryName(projectPath)
                ?? throw new ArgumentException("Cannot resolve project folder.");
            _testCasePath = testCasePath;
        }

        public class BuildTestResult
        {
            public bool BuildSucceeded { get; set; }
            public bool TestsSucceeded { get; set; }
            public string BuildOutput { get; set; } = string.Empty;
            public string BuildErrors { get; set; } = string.Empty;
            public string TestOutput { get; set; } = string.Empty;

            // Errors parsed from build output — used for LLM self-correction
            public List<string> ParsedBuildErrors { get; set; } = new();
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Runs restore → build → test in sequence.
        /// Returns full output for reporting and LLM self-correction.
        /// </summary>
        public async Task<BuildTestResult> RunAsync()
        {
            var result = new BuildTestResult();
            Console.WriteLine("[Test] Running dotnet restore...");
            //await RunDotnetAsync($"clean \"{_projectPath}\"");
            await RunDotnetAsync($"clean \"{_projectPath}\"");
            // ── Step 1: Restore ──────────────────────────────────────────────
            Console.WriteLine("[Test] Running dotnet restore...");
            var (restoreCode, restoreOut, restoreErr) = await RunDotnetAsync(
                $"restore \"{_projectPath}\"");

            
            if (restoreCode != 0)
            {
                Console.WriteLine("[Test] ❌ Restore failed.");
                result.BuildSucceeded = false;
                result.BuildErrors = restoreErr;
                result.BuildOutput = restoreOut;
                return result;
            }

            // ── Step 2: Build MAIN project ─────────────────────────────────────

            //  Get main project path (remove .Tests if present)
            //var mainProjectPath = _projectPath.Replace(".Tests", "");

            //Console.WriteLine("[Test] Running dotnet build (MAIN project)...");
            //var (buildCode, buildOut, buildErr) = await RunDotnetAsync(
            //    $"build \"{mainProjectPath}\" --no-restore --verbosity normal");
            //Console.WriteLine("[Test] Building main project...");
            //var (mainBuildCode, mainBuildOut, mainBuildErr) =
            //    await RunDotnetAsync($"build \"{_projectPath}\"");

            //if (mainBuildCode != 0)
            //{
            //    Console.WriteLine("[Test] ❌ Main project build failed.");
            //    result.BuildSucceeded = false;
            //    result.BuildErrors = mainBuildErr;
            //    result.BuildOutput = mainBuildOut;
            //    return result;   //  STOP here if main build fails
            //}
            Console.WriteLine("[Test] Building main project...");
            var (mainBuildCode, mainBuildOut, mainBuildErr) =
                await RunDotnetAsync($"build \"{_projectPath}\"");

            // 🔁 RETRY LOOP STARTS HERE
            int retry = 0;

            while (retry < 2 && mainBuildCode != 0)
            {
                retry++;
                Console.WriteLine($"[Fix] Attempt {retry} to fix build errors...");

                foreach (var file in Directory.GetFiles(_projectFolder, "*.cs", SearchOption.AllDirectories))
                {
                    var code = File.ReadAllText(file);

                    var fixedCode = await _migrator.SelfCorrectBuildErrorsAsync(
                        code,
                        mainBuildErr,
                        file);

                    if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fixedCode))
                    {
                        File.WriteAllText(file, fixedCode);
                    }
                }

                // 🔁 Rebuild after fix
                Console.WriteLine("[Fix] Rebuilding after fix...");
                (mainBuildCode, mainBuildOut, mainBuildErr) =
                    await RunDotnetAsync($"build \"{_projectPath}\"");
            }

            // ❌ If still failing after retries
            if (mainBuildCode != 0)
            {
                Console.WriteLine("[Fix] ❌ Build still failing after retries.");
                result.BuildSucceeded = false;
                result.BuildErrors = mainBuildErr;
                return result;
            }

            // ✅ Build success
            result.BuildSucceeded = true;

            // ✅ If reached here → build succeeded
            result.BuildSucceeded = true;

            result.BuildOutput = mainBuildOut + mainBuildErr;
            result.BuildSucceeded = mainBuildCode == 0;

            if (!result.BuildSucceeded)
            {
                Console.WriteLine("[Test] ❌ Main project build failed.");
                result.BuildErrors = mainBuildErr;
                result.ParsedBuildErrors = ParseBuildErrors(mainBuildOut + mainBuildErr);
                return result;
            }

            Console.WriteLine("[Test] ✅ Main project build succeeded.");

            // ── Step 3: Resolve test project ─────────────────────────────────
            Console.WriteLine("[Test] Running dotnet test...");

            string? testFolder;

            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_testCasePath))
            {
                // Priority 1: Explicit path supplied in event.json — most authoritative
                testFolder = ResolveTestCsproj(_testCasePath);
                Console.WriteLine($"[Test] Using testCasePath from event.json: {testFolder}");
            }
            else
            {
                // Priority 2: Derive directly from source project name — most reliable
                // e.g. ConsoleApp1.csproj  →  ConsoleApp1.Tests\ConsoleApp1.Tests.csproj
                testFolder = DeriveTestProjectFromSourceProject();

                // Priority 3: Folder-walk fallback only when derive fails
                if (testFolder == null)
                {
                    testFolder = FindTestProject(_projectFolder);
                    Console.WriteLine(
                        testFolder != null
                        ? $"[Test] Auto-discovered test project: {testFolder}"
                        : "[Test] ⚠  No test project found via auto-discovery.");
                }
            }

            if (testFolder == null)
            {
                Console.WriteLine("[Test] ⚠ No test project found. Skipping test run.");
                result.TestsSucceeded = true; // Not a failure — just no tests
                result.TestOutput = "No test project found.";
                return result;
            }

            // ── Step 4: Run tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────
            // ✅ --no-build removed intentionally: the test project is built fresh
            //    here so we never run against a stale or previously-broken DLL.
            Console.WriteLine("[Test] Running dotnet test...");
            //var (testCode, testOut, testErr) = await RunDotnetAsync(
            //    $"test \"{testFolder}\" --no-build --logger console;verbosity=normal");
            var (testCode, testOut, testErr) = await RunDotnetAsync(
                $"test \"{testFolder}\" --logger console;verbosity=normal");
            if (testCode != 0)
            {
                Console.WriteLine("[Fix] Attempting to fix test failures...");

                foreach (var file in Directory.GetFiles(_projectFolder, "*.cs", SearchOption.AllDirectories))
                {
                    var code = File.ReadAllText(file);

                    var fixedCode = await _migrator.SelfCorrectBuildErrorsAsync(
                        code,
                        testErr,
                        file);

                    if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fixedCode))
                    {
                        File.WriteAllText(file, fixedCode);
                    }
                }

                // 🔁 Retry test
                Console.WriteLine("[Fix] Re-running tests...");
                (testCode, testOut, testErr) =
                    await RunDotnetAsync($"test \"{testFolder}\"");
            }

            // ✅ FINAL TEST RESULT SET HERE
            result.TestsSucceeded = testCode == 0;
            result.TestOutput = testOut + testErr;
            result.TestsSucceeded = testCode == 0 && result.BuildSucceeded ;

            Console.WriteLine(result.TestsSucceeded
                ? "[Test] ✅ All tests passed."
                : "[Test] ❌ Some tests failed.");

            return result;
        }

        // ── NEW METHOD ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        /// <summary>
        /// Derives the test project path directly from the source project name.
        /// 
        /// Pattern:
        ///   Source : …\ConsoleApp1\ConsoleApp1.csproj
        ///   Test   : …\ConsoleApp1.Tests\ConsoleApp1.Tests.csproj
        ///
        /// This avoids broad folder-walk discovery that can pick up the wrong
        /// test project (e.g. the pipeline's own test project).
        /// </summary>
        private string? DeriveTestProjectFromSourceProject()
        {
            // "ConsoleApp1"
            var projectName = Path.GetFileNameWithoutExtension(_projectPath);

            // "C:\R\Projects\demoproj\demoproj"
            var parentDir = Path.GetDirectoryName(_projectFolder);
            if (parentDir == null) return null;

            // "C:\R\Projects\demoproj\demoproj\ConsoleApp1.Tests"
            var testProjectDir = Path.Combine(parentDir, $"{projectName}.Tests");

            // "C:\R\Projects\demoproj\demoproj\ConsoleApp1.Tests\ConsoleApp1.Tests.csproj"
            var testProjectFile = Path.Combine(testProjectDir, $"{projectName}.Tests.csproj");

            if (File.Exists(testProjectFile))
            {
                Console.WriteLine($"[Test] ✅ Derived test project: {testProjectFile}");
                return testProjectFile;
            }

            Console.WriteLine($"[Test] ⚠  Derived test project not found at: {testProjectFile}");
            return null;
        }

        private static string? ResolveTestCsproj(string testCasePath)
        {
            // Format 1: already a .csproj file
            if (testCasePath.EndsWith(".csproj", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
                && File.Exists(testCasePath))
                return testCasePath;

            // Format 2: a folder — find the first .csproj inside it
            if (Directory.Exists(testCasePath))
            {
                var found = Directory
                    .GetFiles(testCasePath, "*.csproj", SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly)
                    .FirstOrDefault();

                if (found != null) return found;
            }

            // Could not resolve — return null so caller can warn
            Console.WriteLine(
                $"[Test] ⚠  testCasePath '{testCasePath}' is not a valid " +
                $"folder or .csproj file.");
            return null;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Parses CS error codes from dotnet build output.
        /// e.g.  "error CS1061: 'SqlConnection' does not contain..."
        /// </summary>
        public static List<string> ParseBuildErrors(string buildOutput)
        {
            var errors = new List<string>();
            var lines = buildOutput.Split(
                new[] { '\r', '\n' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);

            foreach (var line in lines)
            {
                if (System.Text.RegularExpressions.Regex.IsMatch(
                    line,
                    @"error CS\d+",
                    System.Text.RegularExpressions.RegexOptions.IgnoreCase))
                {
                    errors.Add(line.Trim());
                }
            }

            return errors;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Fallback: walks up the folder tree (max 4 levels) looking for
        /// a *.Tests.csproj or *Spec*.csproj near a .sln file.
        /// Only used when DeriveTestProjectFromSourceProject() returns null.
        /// </summary>
        private static string? FindTestProject(string projectFolder)
        {
            var searchRoot = projectFolder;

            // Walk up until we find a .sln (solution root) — max 4 levels
            for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++)
            {
                var parent = Directory.GetParent(searchRoot)?.FullName;

                // Stop at drive root (e.g. C:\)
                if (parent == null || parent == Path.GetPathRoot(parent))
                    break;

                searchRoot = parent;

                // Stop at the first folder that contains a solution file
                if (Directory.GetFiles(searchRoot, "*.sln").Length > 0)
                    break;
            }

            try
            {
                var testProjects = Directory
                    .GetFiles(searchRoot, "*.csproj", SearchOption.AllDirectories)
                    .Where(p =>
                        p.Contains("Test", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ||
                        p.Contains("Spec", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                    .Where(p => !p.Contains(
                        $"{Path.DirectorySeparatorChar}obj{Path.DirectorySeparatorChar}"))
                    .ToList();

                return testProjects.FirstOrDefault();
            }
            catch (UnauthorizedAccessException ex)
            {
                Console.WriteLine(
                    $"[Test] ⚠ Could not scan for test projects: {ex.Message}");
                return null;
            }
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Runs a dotnet CLI command and captures stdout + stderr.
        /// </summary>
        private static async Task<(int ExitCode, string StdOut, string StdErr)>
            RunDotnetAsync(string arguments)
        {
            using var process = new Process();
            process.StartInfo = new ProcessStartInfo
            {
                FileName = "dotnet",
                Arguments = arguments,
                RedirectStandardOutput = true,
                RedirectStandardError = true,
                UseShellExecute = false,
                CreateNoWindow = true
            };

            var stdOut = new StringBuilder();
            var stdErr = new StringBuilder();

            process.OutputDataReceived += (_, e) =>
            {
                if (e.Data != null) stdOut.AppendLine(e.Data);
            };
            process.ErrorDataReceived += (_, e) =>
            {
                if (e.Data != null) stdErr.AppendLine(e.Data);
            };

            process.Start();
            process.BeginOutputReadLine();
            process.BeginErrorReadLine();
            await process.WaitForExitAsync();

            return (process.ExitCode, stdOut.ToString(), stdErr.ToString());
        }
    }
}
public async Task<string?> SelfCorrectBuildErrorsAsync(
    string fileContent,
    string buildErrors,
    string filePath)
{
    _output.WriteLine($"[LLM] Self-correcting: {Path.GetFileName(filePath)}...");

    // 🔒 Safety: skip if no errors
    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(buildErrors))
    {
        _output.WriteLine("[LLM-FIX] No build errors provided. Skipping fix.");
        return null;
    }

    var systemPrompt = BuildSelfCorrectionSystemPrompt();
    var userPrompt = BuildSelfCorrectionUserPrompt(fileContent, buildErrors, filePath);

    var requestBody = new
    {
        messages = new[]
        {
            new { role = "system", content = systemPrompt },
            new { role = "user", content = userPrompt }
        },
        temperature = 0.02,
        max_completion_tokens = 8192
    };

    var url = $"{_endpoint}/openai/deployments/{_deployment}/chat/completions?api-version={_apiVersion}";
    var json = JsonSerializer.Serialize(requestBody);
    var httpContent = new StringContent(json, Encoding.UTF8, "application/json");

    // 🔁 Retry logic (NEW)
    for (int attempt = 1; attempt <= MaxRetries; attempt++)
    {
        try
        {
            var response = await _httpClient.PostAsync(url, httpContent);

            if (!response.IsSuccessStatusCode)
            {
                _output.WriteLine($"[LLM-FIX] Attempt {attempt} failed: {response.StatusCode}");
                await Task.Delay(1000 * attempt);
                continue;
            }

            var responseJson = await response.Content.ReadAsStringAsync();

            using var doc = JsonDocument.Parse(responseJson);

            var content = doc.RootElement
                .GetProperty("choices")[0]
                .GetProperty("message")
                .GetProperty("content")
                .GetString();

            var cleaned = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(content)
                ? null
                : StripCodeFences(content);

            // 🔒 Safety check (NEW)
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(cleaned))
            {
                if (!cleaned.Contains("class") && !cleaned.Contains("namespace"))
                {
                    _output.WriteLine("[LLM-FIX] Suspicious output ignored.");
                    continue;
                }

                return cleaned;
            }
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            _output.WriteLine($"[LLM-FIX] Attempt {attempt} exception: {ex.Message}");
        }

        await Task.Delay(1000 * attempt);
    }

    _output.WriteLine("[LLM-FIX] All retries failed.");
    return null;
}
